LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT NOTICE TO DEFENDANT THAT SHE SUFFERED FROM A DISABILITY. THE DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS PER AVILA V. CONTINENTAL AIRLINES, INC. (2008) 165 CAL.APP.4TH 1237. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH HER CLAIM FOR RETALIATION. DEFENDANT PROVIDED A LEGITIMATE REASON FOR THE TERMINATION AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED SUBSTANTIAL RESPONSIVE EVIDENCE SHOWING PRETEXT. THE TIMING OF THE TERMINATION ALONE DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE. (SEE NADAF-RAHROV V. NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC. (2008) 166 CAL.APP.4TH 952, 990). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

Plaintiff Latoya Zeno'S Motion For Relief From Order And Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Karine Bohbot, Esq., of Bohbot & Riles, LLP, Tel. 510.273.3111, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.
Larry Kazanjian, Esq., of Palmer Kazanjian Holden, LLP, Tel. 916.442.3552, appearing for Defendant and opposing party via court call.

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. After hearing, Plaintiff Latoya Zeno'S Motion For Relief From Order And Judgment is taken under submission by Judge Kahn. Each party to submit its own proposed order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

Defendant Blood Centers Of The Pacific'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Larry Kazanjian, Esq., of Palmer Kazanjian Holden, LLP, Tel. 916.442.3552, appearing for Defendant and moving party via court call.
Karine Bohbot, Esq., of Bohbot & Riles, LLP, Tel. 510.273.3111, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court vacated its tentative ruling. Defendant Blood Centers Of The Pacific'S Motion For Summary Judgment is taken under submission. Each party is to submit its own proposed order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Relief From Order And Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Karine Bohbot, Esq., of Bohbot & Riles LLP, Tel. 510.273.3111, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.
Larry M. Kazanjian, Esq., of Palmer Kazanjian Wohl Hodson LLP, Tel. 916.442.3552, appearing for Defendant and opposing party via court call.

RULING: A R G U E D and the court adopted its tentative ruling with modifications. PLAINTIFF LATOYA ZENO's Motion For Relief From Order And Judgment is granted. The court treats the motion as seeking relief from the prior order and, if such relief is granted, reconsideration of the prior order based on Ms. Zeno's declaration. So treated, the motion for relief is granted on the condition that Ms. Zeno pay the reasonable attorney's fees incurred by defendant in opposing this motion and the motion for new trial, neither of which would have been necessary but for the late submission of the declaration. The parties are directed to meet and confer in an effort to reach an agreement as to the amount of those fees and, if they are unable to do so, to advise the court of their differences at the hearing on February 29. Ms. Zeno's failure to submit her declaration on a timely basis was a mistake and/or excusable neglect entitling her to relief. Upon reconsideration of the prior order based on Ms. Zeno's declaration, that declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant had notice of Ms. Zeno's claimed disability and thus the court vacates that prior summary judgment order as to that issue. However, the court does not rule on the related but separate issue of whether the conditions plaintiff notified defendant constitute a 'disability' or 'medical condition' and thus will address this issue at the hearing on February 29. Motion continued to February 29 for determination of the fees to be awarded to defendant.
Defendant's motion for summary judgment/adjudication heard on October 7 and ruled on by order filed October 20 is put back on for hearing on February 29 as a result of the grant of plaintiff's motion for relief from order and judgment for the sole purpose of hearing argument on whether the conditions plaintiff notified defendant about constitute a 'disability' or 'medical condition.' Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Ntc Of Intention To Move For New Trial
Off Calendar Feb-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Karine Bohbot, Esq., of Bohbot & Riles LLP, Tel. 510.273.3111, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.
Larry M. Kazanjian, Esq., of Palmer Kazanjian Wohl Hodson LLP, Tel. 916.442.3552, appearing for Defendant and opposing party via court call.

RULING: A R G U E D and the court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LATOYA ZENO'S Notice Of Intention to Move For A New Trial is off calendar in light of the ruling on plaintiff's motion for relief from order and judgment. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. KATHRYN TANNER OF THE HASSELL LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 334-4111 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KARINE BOHBOT OF THE BOHBOT & RILES, LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 273-3111 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. HEATHER S. CANDY OF THE PALMER/HODSON LLP, TEL. NO. (916) 442-3552 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff did not provide sufficient notice to defendant that she suffered from a disability. The disability discrimination cause of action fails per Avila v. Continental Airlines, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1237. Plaintiff cannot establish her claim for retaliation. Defendant provided a legitimate reason for the termination and plaintiff has not provided substantial responsive evidence showing pretext. The timing of the termination alone does not create a triable issue. (See Nadaf-Rahrov v. Neiman Marcus Group, Inc. (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 952, 990). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G). EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2011 Off Calendar

Plaintiff La Tyoya Zeno'S Notice Of Motion To Quash And/Or Limit Medical Records Subpoenas; And Request For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KARINE BOHBOT OF THE BOHBOT & RILES, LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 273-3111 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. HEATHER S. CANDY OF THE PALMER KAZANJIAN & HODSON LLP, TEL. NO. (916) 442-3552 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Latoya Zeno'S Motion To Quash And/Or Limit Medical Records Subpoenas And Request For Attorney's Fees And Costs is Granted except that documents during the period from October 1 through December 31, 2009 dealing with depression and PTSD must be produced and on condition that Plaintiff not raise any issue with respect to her mental health before or after that time period. The court did not award sanctions. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported = (301/PJB)
